title: Learning Product Outcomes, Command Vocabulary, and Session Contract · Contract Lens: Pokedex Ts Inclusive Cli Contract
summary: A science museum wants a Pokedex kiosk command but has not defined its learner decision, audience needs, command vocabulary, session boundaries, or evidence of understanding. Predict the user decision, runtime value, strict type claim, promise state, API boundary, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex ts inclusive cli contract increment in bounded learner-facing command product charter. Learners progress through predic…
objectives: Make help, progress, errors, and results usable by keyboard, screen readers, high zoom, narrow terminals, and users who cannot rely on color or motion.
Model ready, prompting, parsing, dispatching, waiting, reporting, aborted, closing, and closed states with legal transitions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Pokedex Ts Inclusive Cli Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 5526 during an out-of-order Promise fault rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate a useful inclusive command product on observable learning, lifecycle, and recovery evidence; the release claim must bind source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, and packed artifact; then reorder one Promise, waiter, cache, timeout, or queue settlement and exclude stale state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
